calendar.matters:
Petition For Interpretation Of Trust.
Off Calendar Jun-26-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Rasch for James Lilenthal. Lawrence Siracusa for Thomas Lucas. Seth Skootsky for Peter Lilienthal.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions concerning interpretation to the language of the trust with specific reference to the understanding of the term "necessary", and whether or not the Trustee needs to considered other sources of income available to James. Discussions were held in regards to: Mr. Muhs's assisting in drafting the will, the reason why there is a difference in the distribution to the brothers, and the phrase "absolute discretion". In open Court, all counsel waived tentative ruling by the Court and the Court will issue a proposed statement of decision and issue a statement of decision. The matter is taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Court Reporter: Jaimie Hopp (CSR #: 13751. Telephone #: 415-433-5777). Clerk: Nancy Bautista. Bailiff: Deputy Chien.

Petition For Interpretation Of Trust.
Off Calendar May-07-2013 Continued To May-28-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Interpretation Of Trust.
Off Calendar Apr-23-2013 Continued To May-07-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Interpretation Of Trust.
Off Calendar Apr-02-2013 Continued To Apr-23-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-11-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Seth Skootsky for Peter Lilienthal (Trustee). Lawrence Siracusa for Thomas Lucas (Current-Trustee). Emily Calhoun James Lilienthal.

Mr. Skootsky provided the Court with an endorsed copy of a Third Amended Supplement to The Account he filed this morning in response to the Probate Examiner's notes (previously viewed by Ms. Calhoun and Mr. Siracusa). Ms. Calhoun stated that she was not planning on filing anything further for the reason being the "no contest clause", that the accounting was not of the Sub-Trust but of the Parent-Trust, raised the issue of conflict of interest with Peter being the Trustee and stated that the purported facts on the January 3, 2013 brief mischaracterized historical facts of the Trust. Ms. Calhoun also provided Trust history. Mr. Siracusa stated that his Client filed his objections not because he is taking sides but out of his duties as Trustee, and out of concerns regarding actions of the prior Trustee and the fees (Trustee's and Attorney's). Mr. Siracusa also provided Trust history and addressed fees. Mr. Skootsky stated that Ms. Calhoun's objections were not verified and are improper, provided Trust history and presented oral argument to support the Trustee's fees as well as his Attorney's Fees. Mr. Skootksy also respectfully requested that if there is a reduction in fees, that his fees be reduced but not his Client's. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Whitehead.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION

Petition To Confirm Current Vacancy In The Office Of Independent Trustee, To Confirm Petitioner As Family Trustee, And To Appoint Professional Fiduciary Thomas Lucas To Serve As Independent Trustee
Off Calendar Oct-03-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Emilie Calhoun for and with James Lilientha (Petitioner). Andrew Schultz for Thomas Lucas (Proposed Trustee). Ernest Der for Peter Lilientha.

Ms. Calhoun presented oral argument to support her position with regard to granting relief not set forth in the prayer. Mr. Der stated the Accounting is set for hearing on November 20. Ms. Calhoun raised the issue of the Peremptory Challenge to Judge Wiss and the Court indicated that issue is not before the Court. Mr. Schultz stated that Mr. Lucas has received a check for $350,000.00 and that Ms. Calhoun had told him not to cash it. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Current Vacancy, etc. for relief that is set forth in the prayer and instructed Ms. Calhoun to prepare a revised order with approval as to form.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.

Petition To Confirm Current Vacancy In The Office Of Independent Trustee, To Confirm Petitioner As Family Trustee, And To Appoint Professional Fiduciary Thomas Lucas To Serve As Independent Trustee

Petition For Determination That Proposed Filing Would Not Violate No-Contest Clause
Off Calendar Jan-18-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Emilie Calhoun for James Lilienthal. Seth Skootsky for Peter Lilienthal.

The Court granted the Petition for Determination That Proposed Filing Would Not Violate No-Contest Clause (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.
